title: Decades-old weeping willow collapses in Boston Public Garden
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 68-year-old weeping willow collapsed in the Boston Public Garden after exceeding its typical lifespan.
No injuries were reported from the collapse, which occurred despite previous inspections deeming the tree structurally sound.
The Friends of the Public Garden conduct tree removals in winter and replant in spring, maintaining proactive tree care.

title: These new speed boards in Jamaica Plain, on the Esplanade track cyclists’ speeds, not just cars
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: New speed boards in Jamaica Plain and Charles River Esplanade aim to slow down cyclists and vehicles, says DCR.
The boards gather data on user volume to help officials assess activity on properties like the Southwest Corridor Park.
Additional installations for cyclist monitoring are set along trails including Milton's Neponset River and Cape Cod Rail Trail.

title: Thousands demand Wu address street safety after cyclist killed in crash
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: 4,000 people signed a letter urging Mayor Wu to enhance street safety after cyclist Louisa Gag was killed by a truck.
The letter demands action on stalled safety projects, budget restoration, and oversight of high-crash areas like Tremont Street.
Mayor Wu announced plans for increased enforcement and accelerated street safety project development following Gag's death.

title: Police investigating after man, woman in their 70s found dead in Maine home
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Maine State Police investigating deaths of Carolyn Harris, 77, and Kevin Harris, 75, found in West Bath home.
Sagadahoc County Sheriff's Department discovered them during a welfare check on July 13 on State Road.
Autopsies are being performed; police state there is no threat to the public.

title: Multiple issues after getting an SUV back from the body shop
author: John Paul
bullets: 2024 Honda CR-V EX owner experiences issues with interior lights and automatic headlights after body shop repairs.
Car Doctor suggests possible ADAS system recalibration and faulty programming by the body shop technicians.
Owner advised to contact insurance for a supplemental claim and get the SUV checked by a programming technician.

title: Officers shot at during Dorchester traffic stop, police say, prompting outcry from union
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two Boston police officers were shot at during a Dorchester traffic stop; the suspect fired at them and fled.
Rasiel Joel Carbuccia, 20, was arrested and faces multiple charges, including assault with a dangerous weapon.
The police union called for officials to condemn the attack, emphasizing the need for officer safety and support.

title: Woman, 25, dead, driver and 3 children hospitalized in crash on I-495 in Andover
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 25-year-old woman from Lawrence died in a minivan crash on I-495 North in Andover, police said.
The minivan involved in the crash was carrying two adults and three children with 'unknown injuries,' State Police reported.
The collision involved a sedan with no reported injuries to its driver; the incident is under investigation by State Police.

title: 3 longtime tenants to close or move after Alpha Management buys prominent Allston commercial building
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Alpha Management bought 149-175 Harvard Ave. in Allston for $12.25 million, prompting tenant concerns over rent increases.
Pet Shop, Ritual Arts, and Regeneration Tattoo will vacate as they cannot afford higher rents after the building's ownership change.
Alpha Management plans to renovate the property, offering some tenant relocations within the building at reduced rents.

title: Paul Pierce, Kevin Garnett to introduce Doc Rivers at Hall of Fame ceremony
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Doc Rivers chooses Paul Pierce and Kevin Garnett as enshrinement presenters for Hall of Fame ceremony.
Rivers coached Boston Celtics to their 17th championship in 2008, his only NBA title as head coach.
Rivers ranks sixth on all-time NBA coaching wins list with 1,194 victories across five franchises.

title: Jayson Tatum says it’s ‘weird’ that Jaylen Brown is no longer a Celtic
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum expressed that it's 'weird' Jaylen Brown is now a 76er after nine years as Celtics teammates.
Brown was traded to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George, two first-round picks, and two second-round picks.
The trade elicited strong emotions from Celtics fans, with social media tributes and debates about its implications.
